signals.reason: Leverages a highly rare, low-cost direct Ryanair flight (around €42) from local Plovdiv Airport (Tier 1 transit) to Milan. A short onward train ride connects to Lake Garda for a 5-night stay. This is a high-excitement destination at strong absolute value for August, avoiding the severe friction of a Sofia airport departure while delivering premium Italian lakes summer utility and pool access for a 4-year-old.

Live signals indicate massive late-summer price dumps for Turkish 5-star resorts. While the drive from Plovdiv is Tier 2 (approx 6-7 hours), the staggering depth of the discount (often effectively under €90/night all-inclusive) and the colossal built-in utility of an on-site aquapark and kids' club for a 4-year-old completely offsets the transit friction. A 5-night stay maximizes the value.

A highly convenient 2.5-hour drive (Tier 1) for a perfectly sized 3-night weekend with excellent 5-star indoor kids' facilities. Priced accurately at ~€104/night, this offers a solid, low-friction domestic getaway. Scored below 80 as it represents standard September shoulder-season pricing rather than a steep, actionable arbitrage drop.

An ultra-low friction 1.5-hour drive combined with half-board dining and daily children's animation. While convenient and pleasant for a 4-year-old, the €127/night rate is standard August local pricing without a significant discount framing, keeping it well below the 80-point alert threshold.
